steps.title: Substitute the point into the circle equation
steps.content:
Since $(6, c)$ lies on the circle $(x-3)^2 + (y-5)^2 = 9$, substitute $x = 6$ and $y = c$:
$$(6-3)^2 + (c-5)^2 = 9$$
Simplify:
$$9 + (c-5)^2 = 9$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content:
Subtract 9 from both sides:
$$(c-5)^2 = 0$$
Take the square root:
$$c - 5 = 0$$
$$c = 5$$
